description: A four-pillar adaptive teaching system that turns every game into a personalized lesson. The app watches how you play, identifies your weaknesses, and gradually removes support as you improve. The goal: an expat sits down at a café board and plays without needing the app.

design_philosophy.core_principle: The best teacher makes themselves unnecessary.
design_philosophy.scaffolding_rule: Everything that helps you — dots, labels, hints, nudges — should fade as you prove you don't need it anymore.
design_philosophy.tracking_rule: Track mistakes, not games played. Improvement is measured by errors not made.
design_philosophy.privacy: Everything runs locally. No server. No account. localStorage only.

pillar_1_appropriate_opponent.description: Medium bot with weakened evaluation function that plays realistic human-like moves.

current_implementation.easy: Random legal move bot — sandbox for learning piece flow
current_implementation.medium: Depth 2 minimax with weakened eval weights — makes positional errors, bad trades, slow development. Consistent personality, not random blunders.
current_implementation.hard: Depth 2 minimax with full eval — punishes mistakes immediately

pillar_2_post_game_analysis.description: After every game, identify the 2-3 moments where the player's position deteriorated the most. Show them with plain English explanations.

during_game.step_1: On every move, the AI already evaluates the board position (this is what the minimax does). Save the evaluation score for both before and after each player move.
during_game.step_2: Store as array: [{ move_number, fen_before, fen_after, eval_before, eval_after, piece_moved, move_notation }]
during_game.storage: In-memory during game, persisted to localStorage on game end

after_game.step_1: Calculate eval_drop for each player move: eval_before - eval_after
after_game.step_2: Sort by largest eval_drop to find the 2-3 biggest mistakes
after_game.step_3: Classify each mistake into a category (see mistake_categories below)
after_game.step_4: Present to the player with explanation and the option to replay from that position

mistake_categories.description: Pattern-match the biggest eval drops to human-readable explanations. These don't need to be perfect — approximate is fine for teaching.

categories.detection: Player moved a piece to a square attacked by opponent and not defended
categories.english: You left your {piece_name} unprotected on {square}. {opponent_piece} captured it next move.
categories.example: You left your Horse unprotected on f5. The Cannon captured it next move.

categories.detection: Opponent had an unprotected piece that player didn't capture
categories.english: You missed a free capture — the {piece_name} on {square} was unprotected.
categories.example: You missed a free capture — the Chariot on a7 was unprotected.

categories.detection: Player moved a piece that was between the two Generals on the same file
categories.english: You moved a piece off the file between the Generals. This exposed your General to the Flying General rule.
categories.example: Moving your Horse off the e-file left your General facing the enemy General.

categories.detection: Horse captured in first 6 moves, typically by Cannon
categories.english: Your Horse was captured in the opening. Cannons can attack Horses from move one — develop your Elephants early to block the screen.
categories.example: Your Horse on b1 was taken by the Cannon on b8 using the Knight as a screen.

categories.detection: General left with no Advisor or Elephant support when attacked
categories.english: Your General was exposed. Try to keep at least one Advisor in the palace to block diagonal attacks.
categories.example: Both Advisors were traded off, leaving your General vulnerable to the Cannon.

categories.detection: Player moved a piece into a line where it became a screen for opponent's Cannon
categories.english: You accidentally created a screen for the opponent's Cannon. Watch for Cannons on the same file or rank as your General.
categories.example: Moving your Soldier to e5 gave the enemy Cannon a screen to attack your General.

categories.detection: Elephant captured or player had early positional collapse on their side
categories.english: Your Elephant was captured early. Elephants protect your side of the board — losing them opens diagonal pathways to your General.
categories.example: The enemy Horse took your Elephant, opening a path to the palace.

categories.detection: Player had a forced checkmate in 1-2 moves but didn't find it
categories.english: You missed checkmate in {n} moves! Here's the winning sequence.
categories.example: Your Chariot on e8 could have delivered checkmate by moving to e1.

categories.detection: Large eval drop that doesn't match a specific pattern
categories.english: This move weakened your position significantly. Tap to replay from here and try a different approach.

ui.trigger: After every completed game (win or loss), bottom sheet slides up
ui.prompt_on_loss: 'Here's where things went wrong' — shows 2-3 key moments
ui.prompt_on_win: 'Nice win! Here's one moment you could have played even better'
ui.prompt_on_easy_win: 'Clean game!' — no analysis shown if no significant mistakes

analysis_card.layout: Small board thumbnail showing the position + mistake description text + 'Replay' button
analysis_card.board_thumbnail: Miniature board rendered from the FEN, highlighting the relevant pieces
analysis_card.replay_button: Opens the position in Review Mode (from loss tracker spec) — player can try a different move
analysis_card.save_as_puzzle: If position has a clean tactical solution, offer 'Save as practice puzzle'

ui.dismiss: Swipe down or tap 'New Game' to skip analysis
ui.never_block: Analysis is always optional. Never force the player to review before starting a new game.

pillar_3_pattern_tracking.description: Track recurring mistakes across multiple games. When a pattern emerges, surface it as a personalized lesson and serve targeted puzzles.

schema.occurrences: number — total times this mistake was detected
schema.last_5_games: boolean[] — did this mistake occur in each of the last 5 games
schema.times_shown_to_player: number — how many times we've surfaced this pattern
schema.resolved: boolean — has the player gone 5+ games without this mistake

additional_tracking.track: Number of Horses lost in moves 1-6 per game
Number of times player accidentally created a screen for opponent
Number of times player tried to move a piece pinned by Flying General rule
Whether player develops at least one Elephant in the first 8 moves
Games where player had material advantage but lost or drew

when_to_surface.trigger: After post-game analysis, if a pattern crosses its threshold
when_to_surface.presentation: Additional card in the post-game bottom sheet

format.pattern_alert: 'You've lost a Horse in the opening in {n} of your last {m} games'
format.suggestion: 'Try developing your Elephants before your Horses to block Cannon screens'
format.practice_link: 'Practice this pattern?' — opens a curated or generated puzzle targeting this weakness

targeted_puzzle_serving.description: When a pattern is detected, prioritize puzzles from that category in Practice mode

implementation.step_1: Tag each puzzle in the puzzle library with relevant categories (e.g., 'cannon_screen', 'horse_fork', 'flying_general')
implementation.step_2: When player opens Practice mode, check pattern tracker for active (unresolved) patterns
implementation.step_3: Serve puzzles matching active patterns first, before random puzzles
implementation.step_4: When a pattern is resolved (5 games without the mistake), stop prioritizing those puzzles

pattern_resolution.how: A pattern is marked 'resolved' when the player goes 5 consecutive games without triggering it
pattern_resolution.on_resolved: Brief congratulation: 'You haven't lost a Horse in the opening for 5 games — nice improvement!'
pattern_resolution.can_recur: If the pattern re-emerges after being resolved, it reactivates

pillar_4_scaffolding_that_fades.description: Every visual aid in the app gradually disappears as the player demonstrates they don't need it. The goal is café-readiness: playing without any app assistance.

scaffold_1_legal_move_dots.description: Green dots showing where a selected piece can move

stages.name: Full dots
stages.behavior: All legal moves shown for every piece when selected
stages.visual: Green dots on all legal destination intersections

stages.name: Earned dots
stages.behavior: Dots only shown for piece types the player hasn't mastered yet. Mastered pieces select without dots.
stages.when: Per-piece graduation after consecutive legal moves without illegal attempts
stages.visual: Mastered pieces: selection highlight only, no dots. Unmastered pieces: green dots as normal.
stages.recovery: If player attempts an illegal move with a mastered piece, dots flash back briefly and the piece returns to Stage 1 for that piece type for 3 more games

stages.name: Dots on request
stages.behavior: No dots by default. Long-press a piece to see its legal moves.
stages.when: All 7 piece types have graduated from Stage 2
stages.visual: Clean board on tap. Long-press shows dots with a subtle 'hint' animation.

stages.name: Café mode
stages.behavior: No dots at all. The player sees the board exactly as it looks in real life.
stages.when: Player manually enables in settings, or after extended play in Stage 3 without using long-press hints

per_type.legal_moves_made: number — total moves made with this piece type
per_type.illegal_attempts: number — times player tried an illegal move with this piece type
per_type.consecutive_clean: number — consecutive moves without an illegal attempt
per_type.stage: number — current stage (1-4) for this piece type

graduation_logic.graduation_threshold: 10 consecutive legal moves with a piece type without any illegal attempts
graduation_logic.note: This threshold is per piece TYPE, not per individual piece. All Horses share one tracker, all Cannons share one, etc.

manual_override.settings_toggle: Legal move dots: Always / Adaptive / On Request / Off
manual_override.note: Some days you just want to play relaxed with all the dots. Never lock the player out of assistance.

fade_behavior.stage_1: English labels — default for new players
fade_behavior.stage_2: Vietnamese labels — suggested after completing all 7 lessons or after 20 games
fade_behavior.stage_3: No labels — suggested after player can consistently identify pieces (measured by no mis-taps on wrong piece types for 5 games)

auto_suggestion.trigger: After conditions met, show a one-time toast: 'Ready to try Vietnamese labels?' or 'Ready to play without labels?'
auto_suggestion.player_choice: Always a suggestion, never forced. Player can dismiss and stay on current setting.

scaffold_2_piece_labels.manual_override: Always available in settings toggle: English / Tiếng Việt / Off

scaffold_3_coaching_nudges.description: Gentle warnings during play when the player is about to repeat a known mistake pattern

behavior.when_active: Only triggers for patterns identified by pillar_3 pattern tracking that the player keeps repeating
behavior.what_shows: Subtle toast at bottom: 'Watch your Horse — is it protected?' or 'Check for Cannon screens on that file'
behavior.not_a_hint: Never tells the player what to move. Only draws attention to the danger.
behavior.frequency: Maximum once per game per pattern. Don't nag.

fade_behavior.stage_1: Active nudges — appear when entering a position that matches a tracked pattern
fade_behavior.stage_2: Reduced nudges — after player avoids the mistake 3 times with nudge active, nudge becomes less specific: just a subtle yellow glow on the endangered piece instead of text
fade_behavior.stage_3: No nudges — pattern is resolved (5 games clean). Nudges disappear entirely.
fade_behavior.reactivation: If pattern recurs after resolution, nudges come back at Stage 2, not Stage 1

scaffold_4_post_game_analysis_depth.description: How much analysis is shown after games fades based on player skill

fade_behavior.beginner: Show all 2-3 mistakes with full explanations and suggestions
fade_behavior.improving: Show only the single biggest mistake with a shorter explanation
fade_behavior.experienced: Show 'No major mistakes — nice game!' for clean games. Only flag genuine blunders.
fade_behavior.café_ready: Post-game analysis becomes opt-in rather than automatic

scaffold_4_post_game_analysis_depth.skill_measurement: Based on win rate against Medium over last 10 games. >70% win rate = improving. >85% = experienced.

player_profile.description: Local-only player model that tracks all scaffolding state. No server, no account.

games_history.description: Last 20 games eval history for pattern tracking

café_readiness_meter.description: Optional fun feature — a single progress indicator showing how close the player is to being café-ready.

factors.metric: Percentage of piece types at Stage 3+
Playing with Vietnamese or no labels

café_readiness_meter.display: Small coffee cup icon that fills up. 100% = ready for the café.
café_readiness_meter.location: Settings panel or player profile screen
café_readiness_meter.tone: Encouraging, not pressuring. 'You're getting closer to café-ready! ☕'

implementation_priority.description: Build order based on value and dependencies

phase_1.name: Post-game analysis
phase_1.why_first: Highest value, lowest effort. AI already computes eval — just save it and display after the game.
tasks: Save eval scores during game play
Calculate eval drops on game end
Classify mistakes into categories
Build post-game bottom sheet UI with board thumbnails
Add 'Replay from here' using existing Review Mode

phase_2.name: Scaffolding — legal move dots
phase_2.why_second: Immediately visible improvement to the learning experience. Per-piece tracking is simple.
tasks: Add per-piece-type move tracking to player profile
Implement graduation logic (10 consecutive clean moves)
Modify dot rendering to check piece mastery stage
Add recovery logic (illegal attempt resets to Stage 1)
Add settings toggle override

phase_3.name: Pattern tracking
phase_3.why_third: Needs 5-10 games of history to be useful. By the time phase 1 and 2 are built, you'll have enough game data.
tasks: Implement pattern trackers for each category
Track last 5 games per pattern
Build pattern alert cards in post-game UI
Tag existing puzzles with pattern categories
Prioritize matching puzzles in Practice mode

phase_4.name: Coaching nudges and label fading
phase_4.why_last: Polish layer. The first three pillars do the heavy teaching. This makes it feel seamless.
tasks: Implement in-game nudge system with toast/glow
Connect nudges to pattern tracker
Implement nudge fade stages
Add label mode auto-suggestion based on games played
Build café readiness meter (optional fun feature)
